purpose: This repository implements an AI-powered assistant for loan data analysis in the COS Lending/Selling platform. It uses Amazon Bedrock to provide natural language interactions with structured loan data, enabling users to query loan history, interest calculations, transfers, and other financial details via conversational prompts.
businessFeatures: Loan history timeline visualization and querying

apis.purpose: Submit a natural language question about loan data
Create or resume a chat session for a user+loan pair
Submit a user message to an existing chat session
Get all messages for a specific chat session
Stream chat responses using Server-Sent Events (SSE)

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Core loan information including ID, amounts, dates, and status

dataEntities.name: LoanAction
dataEntities.description: Record of actions performed on a loan

dataEntities.name: LoanEvent
dataEntities.description: Events in a loan's lifecycle including status changes and purchases

dataEntities.name: Transfer
dataEntities.description: Financial transfers related to loans like interest payments and fees

dataEntities.name: InterestCalculationMethod
dataEntities.description: Method used to calculate interest for a loan

dataEntities.name: Session
dataEntities.description: User conversation session with message history

dataEntities.name: FedDay
dataEntities.description: Calendar of federal banking days for interest calculations

messagingPatterns.name: SSE Streaming
messagingPatterns.description: Server-sent events for streaming AI responses to clients

findings.title: SQL Injection Vulnerability
findings.description: Although there is SQL validation logic in sql_executor.py that checks for forbidden keywords and only allows SELECT statements, the application generates SQL from LLM output which could potentially be manipulated. The system should use parameterized queries consistently and add additional validation layers.

findings.title: Connection Pool Management Issues
findings.description: The SQL executor creates a new connection pool without proper lifecycle management, which could lead to connection leaks. The fallback _SimplePool implementation is particularly problematic as it doesn't properly handle connection closing in all error cases.

findings.title: Hardcoded AWS Resource ARNs
findings.description: There appear to be hardcoded AWS resource ARNs in the code that should be moved to configuration. This is evident in the sql_executor code where it references DB_CLUSTER_ARN and DB_SECRET_ARN.

findings.title: Token Validation Without Proper JWK Verification
findings.description: The authentication code doesn't show proper JWT verification against a JWK set from the identity server, which could allow forged tokens if implemented incorrectly. The authentication_code_bearer.py file needs review.
